Determine how you will finance each stage of the business. It's very very important because a lot of times the businesses fail because they just rent out of money. There's a huge failure rate in the restaurant industry not because people make bad food or they have a bad location but they only raised $150,000 or $112,000 and they bootstrapped it to make it and the restaurant actually does 20, 30, 40 grand a month in revenue but they're always playing catch-up and in that one bad month let's say in December when it's raining outside for three weeks and snowstorms and nobody comes in well that three weeks kills them because they were living month to month and all of a sudden their business fails. When they really could have made it if they just had that other 50 grand or 100 grand ahead of time they could have survived those winter storms and had that amazing February, March, April whatever that season is for them. So why it's really important is you want to plan for setting up shop, getting your corporation, your trademark, your legal, your design, everything about your business but then you want to plan out the funding you need for month three, month six, month nine, month 12, planning that out for the first year because it's very critical even when at the first three to nine months of the business, even when you have revenue not a penny that's going to you you're gonna always be reinvesting it. Even when year one is going on so much of that revenue just going back into the business that's very important for you to plan to have enough money that no matter what happens a lot of times you're gonna start getting offers that you never thought of. You're gonna start building a business and you do 50 grand sales then you did 100 grand then you start doing great you do it a quarter million in sales in the first four months like this is awesome however I should also be doing advertising with Google ads oh I should spend money 10 grand with Facebook ads oh I should be spending money on SEO oh I need to hire a PR firm I need to hire a lawyer I need to expand all of a sudden your successful business starts costing more and more and more so your own success is actually a big expense to you you can't say no because you need those things if you're spending a thousand bucks a month on Facebook and it's creating 4,000 well you got to start spending 5,000 or 10,000 and ramp up right but at the same time you have to have more inventory to sell those products that you're selling through Facebook for example in order to do that you have to float that money to get the let's just say t-shirts you're gonna spend 20 grand to buy those t-shirts have the five grants around the Facebook ads you know you're gonna then sell 60,000 in t-shirts this is gonna be really exciting but then you have to you have a 30-day delay on when the guys are paying you and you just have this ever-revolving financial situation to prevent that you want to be prepared ahead of time and you want to have an additional source of revenue especially in the first year of business I always recommend people have either a side job or they're consulting or they're being an expert in the space or they're speaking at events or they're doing side jobs or they're being an Uber driver or bartender I don't care having other sides of income other scenarios of income or an eBay store or an Amazon store is very critical because you will always need to reinvest the money especially the first year back into the business so you can't assume even if you do half a million dollars in sales the first year well out of half a million dollars in sales your net profits are probably gonna be 100 to 200 grand you're gonna have other staff members you got to pay you're gonna have taxes to pay on that and by the time you really break that down you want to want to spend 50 grand 100 grand on more marketing you got to put that back in the business because it's going so well that's why you did half a million sales and you left with nothing it's great for the long run because you've built something that did half a million in sales but you have to keep reinvesting the company almost every single time the first year so you want to have an additional source of income coming in and not be planning on pulling money out of the business during the most critical stages
